'''Solanus''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Poster" /> in the [[Solanus system/Legends|Solanus system]]. It was situated within the [[Dominus sector/Legends|Dominus sector]],<ref name="Online Companion" /> a part of [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="Slice" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Online Companion" /> Solanus was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to a [[Solanus's sentient ocean|sentient ocean]] that [[Waterworld/Legends|covered]] it, a murky [[Ocean/Legends|sea]] so rich in chemicals that it could perform billions of [[Sentience/Legends|thought]] processes within its depths, which classified it as a [[super-brain]]. However, like other super-[[Brain/Legends|brains]], the ocean was too large to move around independently and kept to itself, often contemplating its own existence. It refrained from participating in [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] affairs, instead spending its [[Time/Legends|time]] playing with itself by shaping and reforming intricate [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] structures.<ref name="Poster" />

By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="date">The events of "[[Cantina Communications]]" take place during [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Ben Kenobi's]] visit to [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], which is depicted in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars:'' Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] and dated to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], a young<ref name="Poster" /> [[Gender/Legends|man]] from the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="Databank Luke">{{DB|character|lukeskywalker|Skywalker, Luke}}</ref> had seen pictures of the "thinking" ocean that existed on Solanus, believing it to be one of the strangest examples of the large and complex brains known as super-brains.<ref name="Poster" />

==Behind the scenes==
Solanus and its thinking ocean were created by [[John Chesterman]] for "[[Cantina Communications]]," a one-page vignette included in ''[[Star Wars Official Poster Monthly 16]]'', published in [[1979]] by [[Paradise Press]]. In this printing of "Cantina Communications," the name "Solanus" is not bolded, unlike the surrounding text.<ref name="Poster" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Solanus system, and therefore the planet itself, in grid square U-7.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

Solanus shares similarities with Solaris, a planet covered in a sentient ocean from [[Wikipedia:Stanisław Lem|Stanisław Lem's]] [[Wikipedia:Solaris (novel)|science-fiction novel of the same name]], which was later adapted into a [[Wikipedia:Solaris (1972 film)|film]] by [[Wikipedia:Andrei Tarkovsky|Andrei Tarkovsky]].<ref name="Solaris">{{WebCite|url=https://www.theguardian.com/books/2011/jun/15/first-direct-translation-solaris|text=First ever direct English translation of Solaris published|author=Flood, Alison|date=2011-06-15|work=[https://www.theguardian.com/international ''The Guardian'' International edition]|archivedate=20200325181816}}</ref>
